articles.title: Gränges H1 2026 interim results: operating profit exceeds expectations
articles.summary: Gränges AB published its January–June 2026 interim report today, with operating profit exceeding analyst expectations. The result follows a record Q1 2026 in which EBIT rose 12% year-on-year to SEK 459M on 5.4% volume growth, driven by automotive and specialty wins across Europe, Americas, and Asia.
articles.novelis_angle: Gränges is Novelis's principal competitor in automotive heat-exchanger strip; an H1 beat signals that automotive aluminium sheet demand is recovering more strongly than consensus expected, with positive read-across for Novelis's European automotive volumes.

articles.title: Trimet adds 80,000 t/yr of German aluminium recycling capacity with hydrogen-rich furnaces
articles.summary: Trimet has expanded its Gelsenkirchen smelter with up to 80,000 t/year of new secondary aluminium melting capacity; hydrogen-rich coke-oven gas furnaces are scheduled for operation in August 2026, significantly reducing CO₂ intensity versus natural gas. The programme also adds a 16,000t scrap storage and laser alloy-sorting hall at Essen and a greenfield scrap processing facility in Hamm targeting year-end 2026 commissioning.
articles.novelis_angle: Trimet’s scaled-up secondary capacity intensifies competition for post-consumer and industrial scrap in Germany, directly tightening feedstock availability for Novelis’s Nachterstedt recycling complex.

articles.title: LME aluminium cash gains 1% on July 14 as stocks slip to 286,225t; Iran maritime blockade resumes
articles.summary: LME aluminium cash bid rose approximately 1% on 14 July to USD 3,169/t as total warehouse stocks fell to 286,225t — down 43% year-to-date and below 300,000t for the first time since 2022. US forces resumed the maritime blockade of Iranian ports at 4pm EDT on 14 July; Persian Gulf producers account for approximately 10% of global primary aluminium output.
